intro: Mora ekmanii, also known as Ekman's club moss, is a rare and endemic species of plant belonging to the genus Mora and family Lycopodiaceae, which is a group of ancient, vascular plants that reproduce via spores. Research has shown that Mora ekmanii exhibits potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine for treating various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Mora ekmanii include alkaloids, lignans, and phenolic compounds, which have been identified as the primary drivers of its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Mora ekmanii is often prepared as a tincture, decoction, or capsule, and is used to treat conditions such as arthritis, fever, and respiratory infections.
